topics.title: Osmosis
content: Osmosis is the diffusion of water molecules through a partially (selectively) permeable membrane.
Water moves from a region of higher water concentration (dilute solution / low solute) to a region of lower water concentration (concentrated solution / high solute).
Osmosis is a passive process, no energy is required.
- Hypotonic: solution with lower solute concentration than the cell → water enters cell
- Hypertonic: solution with higher solute concentration than the cell → water leaves cell
- Isotonic: equal solute concentration → no net water movement
In plant cells: osmosis creates turgor pressure (important for support). In animal cells: can cause swelling or shrinking.

questions.question: What is osmosis?
options: Movement of any particles from high to low concentration
Movement of water across a partially permeable membrane from dilute to concentrated solution
Active transport of solutes using energy
Movement of gases across the cell membrane
questions.answer: Movement of water across a partially permeable membrane from dilute to concentrated solution
questions.hint: Osmosis is special because it's only about WATER and uses a semi-permeable membrane.

questions.question: In which direction does water move during osmosis?
options: From high solute concentration to low solute concentration
From low water concentration to high water concentration
From high water concentration (low solute) to low water concentration (high solute)
questions.answer: From high water concentration (low solute) to low water concentration (high solute)
questions.hint: Water always moves to where there are more solutes (to dilute them).
